title: How to Take Better Photos

segments.text: You don't need an expensive camera to take great photos.
The most important thing is light.
Try to shoot during the golden hour — the hour after sunrise or before sunset.
Imagine your image divided into a three-by-three grid and place your subject on one of the lines.
Get closer than you think you need to.

transcript: You don't need an expensive camera to take great photos. The most important thing is light. Try to shoot during the golden hour — the hour after sunrise or before sunset. Use the rule of thirds. Imagine your image divided into a three-by-three grid and place your subject on one of the lines. Get closer than you think you need to. And keep practicing.
